The earth shakes beneath my sandals, and I must hold my shield high against the falling stones.
Let the heavens crack and the fire rain down; I shall meet this chaos with my blade drawn.
This storm is a beast more savage than any lion I have faced in the sands of the arena.
I will carve a path through the rubble to reach the gates before the flood consumes the city.
My armor is heavy and the wind howls, but I will not kneel before the fury of nature.
My shield is heavy, but I will keep it raised against the falling fire from the heavens.
The crowd has gone silent, staring up at the bleeding sky instead of cheering for my kill.
I do not know if this celestial omen is a sign from the gods or a new weapon.
My sandals grip the blood-soaked sand as I watch the stars shift and swirl in madness.
If the world ends now, I shall meet the void with my gladius drawn and chest out.
My trident is ready to carve the line of this soil in blood.
If you will not move your markers, I shall carve them into your chest.
The Lanista does not pay me to argue over dirt, only to slay.
Step back from this boundary or I will show you the Colosseum's mercy.
I fight for my freedom, and I will kill any man who blocks my path.
My sandals grip the sand, but this shimmering void offers no footing for a fighter.
I tighten the straps of my manica and raise my shield against this swirling light.
Does the Lanista intend for me to fight beasts within this glowing circle of madness?
I will step through this portal and carve my name into the glory of a new arena.
The crowd is silent, yet the humming of this gateway sounds like a thousand screaming fans.
My gladius is useless against these thick vines that snag my armor.
I must find a clearing where I can actually swing my shield.
The humidity is rusting the joints of my greaves and slowing me.
I search for a sturdy enemy to face rather than this silence.
My sandals were made for the sand, not this damp, rotting soil.
The crowd has vanished and the roar of the colosseum has faded into this heavy, crushing quiet.
I stand motionless in the sand, listening for the clash of steel that never comes.
My armor feels heavier now that there is no shouting to drive my blood forward.
The silence is a different kind of opponent, one that cannot be cut by my gladius.
I wait in this void, wondering if the editor has forgotten me in the darkness.
Tighten the grip on my gladius and let the sand soak up the blood.
I will drive this trident through your chest before the crowd screams for death.
Step forward and see if your shield can withstand the weight of my blow.
The roar of the Colosseum drowns out your threats, but my blade hears them.
I fight for my freedom today, and your life is the price of it.
This gladius has tasted the blood of ten champions and never once chipped.
My shield is scarred by a hundred blows, yet it keeps my heart beating.
I polish this helmet until the sun blinds my enemies before the first strike.
No other man in the ludus possesses a net as strong as my own.
This leather manica is the only thing saving my arm from the trident's teeth.
My water skin is nearly dry, and the midday sun beats down upon the sand.
I must conserve my strength for the final blow, as my breath grows short.
This rusted gladius will not hold an edge much longer if I strike blindly.
There is only one cup of wine left for the three of us tonight.
I have to choose which wound to bind with the last strip of linen.
